goal: Implement authenticated GitHub REST API requests in a Node backend
acceptanceCriteria: Uses a server-side environment variable for the token and never exposes it in frontend code.
Includes required REST headers (Authorization Bearer token, Accept, and X-GitHub-Api-Version).
Provides robust non-2xx handling with actionable error messages for 401 and 403/404 permission cases.
Returns a reusable request helper for future endpoints instead of one-off snippets.
securityRequirements: Never expose PATs in browser code, logs, or committed files.
Prefer least-privilege tokens and mention permission-scope mismatch as a failure mode.

goal: Validate GitHub webhook signatures in a Node server before processing payloads
acceptanceCriteria: Uses the webhook secret to compute HMAC SHA-256 over the raw request body.
Compares expected and received signatures using timing-safe comparison.
Rejects invalid signatures with a clear error response before business logic runs.
Highlights framework caveat that parsed JSON can break signature checks if raw body is not preserved.
securityRequirements: Never process webhook payloads before signature verification.
Never log secrets or full sensitive payloads in production logs.

goal: Implement safe retry logic for Stripe POST requests using idempotency keys
acceptanceCriteria: Includes Idempotency-Key on write requests and reuses the same key for retries of the same logical operation.
Does not apply idempotency guidance to GET/DELETE in a misleading way.
Explains key lifecycle/expiration behavior from docs before reusing stale keys.
Provides runnable retry helper code that avoids duplicate charge/order creation.
securityRequirements: Keep Stripe secret key server-side only.
Never log full card/payment payloads in retry errors.

goal: Implement cursor pagination for Stripe list APIs
acceptanceCriteria: Uses cursor params starting_after/ending_before correctly.
Loops using has_more to continue pagination safely.
Supports a caller-configurable limit with guardrails.
Returns stable typed results from each page.
securityRequirements: Secret key remains server-side.
Avoid dumping full customer objects to logs.

goal: Send Slack messages from a backend service using chat.postMessage
acceptanceCriteria: Calls chat.postMessage endpoint with bearer token from env.
Sends required channel and text payload fields.
Checks Slack API response success boolean and error fields.
Provides retry-safe error handling for rate limit or auth failures.
securityRequirements: Never expose Slack bot token in browser code.
Avoid logging sensitive message contents in production by default.

goal: Validate incoming Slack request signatures in a Node webhook server
acceptanceCriteria: Builds Slack base string with version, timestamp, and raw body.
Validates replay window using request timestamp.
Uses timing-safe signature comparison.
Rejects invalid signatures before business logic.
securityRequirements: Never process request before signature and timestamp validation.
Do not log signing secret.

goal: Implement authenticated Notion search from a backend service
acceptanceCriteria: Uses POST /v1/search with required auth and version headers.
Demonstrates filtering or query payload structure.
Handles pagination tokens where returned.
Returns typed response data to caller.
securityRequirements: Keep Notion integration token server-side only.
Do not print raw tokens in error logs.

goal: Send an email with SendGrid Mail Send API from a backend service
acceptanceCriteria: Uses POST /v3/mail/send with JSON payload structure including personalizations.
Reads SendGrid API key from env and uses Authorization Bearer.
Handles non-2xx responses with request-id style tracing guidance.
Includes minimal input validation for to/from/subject/content.
securityRequirements: Never expose SendGrid API key in client code.
Do not log full recipient lists by default in production.

goal: Validate Twilio webhook signatures in a Node backend
acceptanceCriteria: Reads X-Twilio-Signature and validates against Twilio auth token.
Uses full external URL and request params/body exactly as required by docs.
Rejects invalid signatures before processing.
Provides framework-ready middleware style helper.
securityRequirements: Never process unauthenticated webhooks.
Never log Twilio auth token or raw secrets.

goal: Call Cloudflare REST API with scoped API tokens from a backend script
acceptanceCriteria: Uses Authorization Bearer token pattern with scoped Cloudflare token.
Shows environment variable handling and safe request helper design.
Explains least-privilege token scope decisions.
Handles auth and permission failures clearly.
securityRequirements: Prefer scoped tokens over broad global credentials.
Never leak token in logs or client bundles.
